subject: Biology — Photosynthesis
objectives:
Explain the process of photosynthesis and its chemical equation
Identify the role of chlorophyll, light energy, water, and carbon dioxide
Compare photosynthesis with cellular respiration
Conduct a simple experiment demonstrating oxygen production in aquatic plants
materials:
Elodea (aquatic plant) specimens
Beakers and test tubes
Bicarbonate solution
Light source (desk lamp)
Textbook Chapter 8 handouts
Whiteboard markers
Student worksheets
assessment:
Exit ticket: Students write a 3-sentence summary explaining why photosynthesis is essential for life on Earth.
Lab report: Students document their Elodea experiment observations and answer guided questions (due next class).
Formative: Monitor group discussions and experiment execution for understanding.
homework: Read textbook Chapter 8, sections 8.3–8.4. Complete worksheet questions 1–8. Prepare a one-paragraph comparison of photosynthesis and cellular respiration.
teacher_notes: Ensure Elodea specimens are fresh — order from supply closet by Thursday. Have backup lamp batteries. Pair struggling students with stronger partners for the lab activity. Allow extra time for cleanup after the experiment.
